---
title: "Erin McIntyre's CV"
author: Erin McIntyre
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pagedown::html_resume:
    css: ['css/styles.css', 'resume']
    # set it to true for a self-contained HTML page but it'll take longer to render
    self_contained: true
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  results='asis', 
  echo = FALSE
)

library(glue)
library(tidyverse)

# Set this to true to have links turned into footnotes at the end of the document
PDF_EXPORT <- FALSE

# Holds all the links that were inserted for placement at the end
links <- c()

source('parsing_functions.R')

# Load csv with position info
position_data <- read_csv('positions.csv')
```


```{r}
# When in export mode the little dots are unaligned, so fix that. 
if(PDF_EXPORT){
  cat("
  <style>
  :root{
    --decorator-outer-offset-left: -6.5px;
  }
  </style>")
}
```


Aside
================================================================================


![logo](beta_dist.png){width=100%}

```{r}
# When in export mode the little dots are unaligned, so fix that. 
if(PDF_EXPORT){
  cat("View this CV online with links at _nickstrayer.me/cv_")
} else {
  cat("[<i class='fas fa-download'></i> Download a PDF of this CV](https://github.com/nstrayer/cv/raw/master/strayer_cv.pdf)")
}
```

Contact {#contact}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


- <i class="fa fa-envelope"></i> ekmcintyre99@gmail.com
- <i class="fa fa-facebook"></i> Erin McIntyre
- <i class="fa fa-github"></i> github.com/ekmcintyre
- <i class="fa fa-link"></i> [ekmcintyre.me](https://ekmcintyre.github.io/)
- <i class="fa fa-phone"></i> (973) 493-xxxx



Language Skills {#skills}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
skills <- tribble(
  ~skill,               ~level,
  "R",                  3.5,
  "Javascript (d3.js)", 0,
  "Python",             0,
  "Bash",               0,
  "SQL",                0,
  "C++",                0,
  "AWK",                0
)
build_skill_bars(skills)
```





Disclaimer {#disclaimer}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Made with the R package [**pagedown**](https://github.com/rstudio/pagedown). 

The source code is available at [github.com/nstrayer/cv](https://github.com/nstrayer/cv).

Last updated on `r Sys.Date()`.




Main
================================================================================

Erin McIntyre {#title}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


```{r}
intro_text <- "I have made graphs, cleaned and analyzed readable text, worked with loops, and analyzed census data using R.

Currently searching for a data science position that allows me to work on meaningful data analysis projects. 
"

cat(sanitize_links(intro_text))
```



Education {data-icon=graduation-cap data-concise=true}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
print_section(position_data, 'education')
```



Research Experience {data-icon=laptop}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
print_section(position_data, 'research_positions')
```



<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>


Industry Experience {data-icon=suitcase}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: aside
I am just beginning my career in data analytics. However, I am a fast
learner and a hard worker. I have also acquired supreme exposure to data
analytics in my college degree programs and research experience.
:::

```{r}
print_section(position_data, 'industry_positions')
```

<br>
<br>
<br>

Teaching Experience {data-icon=chalkboard-teacher}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: aside
I have a lot of experience in management through education in college,
directing and choreographing experience through Parks and Rec. and my community theater, and managing a first grade classroom.
:::

```{r}
print_section(position_data, 'teaching_positions')
```


Selected Data Science Writing {data-icon=chart-line}
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


::: aside
This section features class projects.
:::

```{r}
print_section(position_data, 'data_science_writings')
```
